objective: Make the LifeMax public app visibly more functional and credible by adding browser-local history insights from archived local days, preserving truth boundaries, proving the flow locally and in production, and checkpointing remaining backend/shared-context blockers.
riskClass: Q2 app/frontend product-functionality upgrade with Q3 production deployment already approved by PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote
completion_blocker: The public app slice is deployed and smoke-tested, but the separate LifeMax lab/shared-context boot packet remains red, so this cannot be claimed as full LifeMax daily usability.

independent_verification.verdict: PASS as CHECKPOINT; FAIL as COMPLETE
independent_verification.summary: Verifier reran checks and a production mobile day-loop smoke on both public aliases, accepted the history-insights slice, found no fake backend-readiness claims, and blocked completion because the June 20 lab/shared-context boot packet is red.

repo.feature_commit: de49e41 feat: add local history insights

changes.summary: Adds derived browser-local history_insight states: empty, checkpoint, emerging, trend. True trend now requires at least three recent checkpoints, repeated cue evidence, enough evidence, and enough captures.
Surfaces a History cue in Today, plus a Local history insight panel in Patterns and Profile.
Adds compact, mobile-safe styles for history insight metrics and callout copy.
Covers empty, one-checkpoint, emerging, and trend history insight derivation and export status.
Covers visible insight panel, refresh persistence, Today history cue, Profile summary/export, and screenshots.

functional_flows_proven: Today command center shows an actionable browser-local History cue.
Patterns can save a review checkpoint, archive a local day, and show 'One checkpoint, not a trend yet'.
History insight persists after refresh through browser-local storage.
Profile shows History insight in local data summary and export preview records history_insight status.
Privacy route is reachable from app navigation and deployed aliases.
truth_boundaries_preserved: No WHOOP, account, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, or backend write is claimed.
History insight is derived from browser-local archives only.
One checkpoint is explicitly not a trend.
Two checkpoints are an emerging signal, not a trend.
Trend language requires at least three recent local checkpoints with repeated cue evidence and supporting local captures.

checks.details: 2 Playwright visual tests passed after final cleanup
No matches for common token/secret patterns
Initial run failed missing objective/riskClass; proof packet repaired and rerun passed.

observed: Today command center contained History cue
Patterns rendered Local history insight empty state

loop_evidence.result: Found public repo clean at start and lab preflight/boot packet red.
Added derived history insights and visible panels.

defects_found: Trend label was too eager at two archives.
Today originally gained only a shallow status chip.
repairs_made: Added emerging state and required stronger evidence for trend.
Moved Today surface to an actionable History cue in the command center.

loop_evidence.result: Reran fast checks, final screenshot flow, local in-app smoke, production deploy, production alias smoke.

residual_risks: This remains browser-local demo functionality only.
No real backend, WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, or Postgres integration was connected or verified.
Lab boot/preflight remains red and blocks full LifeMax daily-usability completion.
Vercel reported 2 moderate npm audit findings during remote install; no dependency changes were made in this slice.
next_exact_action: Continue either by adding another visible local product flow or by opening the separate lab shared-context/MCP service repair lane until the LifeMax boot packet is green.
